title: coincya monensis subsp. cheiranthos
intro: Coincya monensis subsp. cheiranthos, also known as Corn Marigold or Gladwin's Curse, is a biennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. Coincya monensis subsp. cheiranthos is often prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
